prediction.attackType: T1505.003 Server Software Component: Web Shell
prediction.summary: An attacker from IP 10.20.8.77 successfully uploaded a web shell to the webroot of 'webserver01'. The attacker then used the web shell to execute commands, including 'whoami', 'cat /etc/passwd', and attempted to download a secondary payload from 'evil.tld'. This activity was detected by WAF, IDS, and SIEM, indicating a critical compromise.

timeline.event: Suspicious PHP file 'shell.php' uploaded via POST request to /uploads/shell.php.
WAF alerts on suspicious PHP upload to webroot /uploads/shell.php.
Attacker executes 'whoami' command via the web shell.
Attacker executes 'cat /etc/passwd' command via the web shell.
Attacker attempts to download 'stage2' from 'http://evil.tld/stage2' via 'wget' command.
IDS detects web shell activity: repeated POST to .php with cmd parameter.
SIEM identifies MITRE T1505.003 Server Software Component: Web Shell.
ModSecurity blocks a SQLi probe from 10.20.8.77 targeting /uploads/shell.php.
SIEM issues CRITICAL alert for webshell on host webserver01 from 10.20.8.77.

recommendations: Immediately isolate the affected web server (webserver01) from the network to prevent further compromise or lateral movement.
Remove the malicious web shell file '/uploads/shell.php' and any other unauthorized files from the server.
Block the source IP address '10.20.8.77' at the perimeter firewall or WAF.
Conduct a full forensic analysis of the compromised server to determine the initial compromise vector, extent of access, and any data exfiltration.
Identify and patch the vulnerability that allowed the web shell upload (e.g., insecure file upload functionality, outdated CMS, misconfigured server).
Perform a threat hunt across the environment for connections to 'evil.tld' or the 'stage2' payload to identify other potentially compromised systems.
